---
title: "Project 2 Proposal"
---

| | |
|---|---|
| **Project** | Project 2: Logistic Regression |
| **Due** | Week 12 |
| **Weight** | 30% of Project 2 (which is 20% of final grade) |
| **Related Guide** | [Project Guide]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} |

::: {.callout-tip}
## {{< fa book >}} Project Guide
Refer to the [Project Guide]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} for detailed instructions, examples of strong proposals, and tips for choosing a dataset with a binary outcome.
:::

## Purpose

The proposal establishes the foundation for your logistic regression project. You will identify a research question involving a binary outcome, select a dataset, and define your variables. The instructor will provide feedback to help you refine your plan.

## Requirements

Your proposal should include the following sections:

### 1. Research Question

State a clear, focused research question that can be addressed with logistic regression. Your question should involve predicting or explaining a **binary outcome** (e.g., pass/fail, yes/no, admitted/rejected).

### 2. Dataset Description

- Name and source of the dataset
- How to access the dataset (URL, R package, etc.)
- Number of observations and a brief description of the sample
- Unit of analysis

### 3. Variable Definitions

Identify and describe the following:

- **Outcome variable** ($y$): A binary variable suitable for logistic regression
- **Primary predictor** ($x_1$): The main explanatory variable of interest
- **Additional predictors**: At least one additional predictor or control variable

For each variable, report:

- Variable name in the dataset
- Measurement scale (continuous, categorical, binary)
- Brief description of what it measures
- For the outcome variable: the coding scheme (what does 0 vs. 1 represent?)

### 4. Preliminary Descriptive Statistics

Provide basic descriptive statistics for your key variables:

- Frequency table for the binary outcome (counts and percentages)
- Summary statistics for continuous predictors
- Frequency tables for categorical predictors
- At least one visualization (bar chart, mosaic plot, or grouped comparison)

## Submission

Submit the following to Blackboard:

- A rendered PDF or HTML document
- Your `.qmd` source file
- The dataset (or a link to it)
